_frame_metadata.forClaude: Check this file to understand what tasks are pending, in progress, or completed. Update task status as you work. Add new tasks when discovered during development. Follow the task recognition rules in CLAUDE.md. IMPORTANT: Include userRequest (original user prompt), detailed description, and acceptanceCriteria for each task.

taskSchema._comment: This schema shows the expected structure for each task
taskSchema.title: Short actionable title (max 60 chars)
taskSchema.description: Claude's detailed explanation - what, how, which files affected
taskSchema.acceptanceCriteria: When is this task done? Concrete testable criteria

pending.title: Session-based prompt logging
pending.description: Log prompts only when Claude is active

pending.title: Claude Code chat sidebar
pending.description: Dedicated UI for Claude Code interactions

pending.title: PROJECT_NOTES.md kurallarini belirle
pending.description: PROJECT_NOTES.md icin guncelleme kurallari ve CLAUDE.md'ye eklenecek detayli talimatlari belirle

pending.title: README.md kurallarini belirle
pending.description: README.md icin guncelleme kurallari ve CLAUDE.md'ye eklenecek detayli talimatlari belirle

pending.title: Improve description parsing in structure script
pending.description: update-structure.js currently parses file descriptions as single letters. Fix extractDescription() to properly extract JSDoc comments and multi-line descriptions.

pending.title: STRUCTURE.json function call graph
pending.description: Add function call relationships to STRUCTURE.json (e.g., functionA calls functionB). This creates a dependency tree that helps Claude navigate code more efficiently.

pending.title: Detect if Claude Code is actually running
pending.description: Currently we track claudeCodeRunning with a simple boolean. Improve detection to actually check if Claude Code is running in terminal (parse terminal output for Claude prompt patterns).

pending.title: Local proxy for model routing (cost + limit optimization)
pending.description: Build a local HTTP proxy server that Frame starts on launch and injects via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL when spawning Claude Code. The proxy intercepts API requests, analyzes the message content, and routes to the appropriate model: simple tasks (file reads, searches, short questions) → Haiku, code writing/editing → Sonnet, architecture/complex features → Opus. Routing decision can be made via a fast Haiku classification call or rule-based heuristics (token count, task category from active tasks.json, keywords). Files affected: src/main/modelRouter.js (new), src/main/ptyManager.js (inject env var), src/main/index.js (start proxy on app launch), settings panel for configuration.
pending.userRequest: User said: ben acaba senin içine bir katman koyabilir miyim. basit işleri mesela çok ucuz bir modele gitmeni sağlayabilir miyim? — motivated by both API cost savings and subscription usage limits (hitting weekly limit means 3h wait; routing simple tasks to Haiku preserves credits for complex work).
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Frame starts a local proxy on launch. ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL is set to proxy when Claude Code is spawned. Simple requests are routed to Haiku, complex ones to Sonnet/Opus. Works with both API key and subscription auth. User can configure routing rules or disable in settings.
pending.notes: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL is the interception point — Claude Code respects this env var. For subscription users the benefit is usage limit preservation, not token cost. Auth forwarding (OAuth tokens for subscription) needs careful handling. Rule-based routing is simpler and more predictable than a learned router. Could use active task category/priority from tasks.json as a routing signal.

pending.title: Click file in tree -> run cat command
pending.description: When user clicks a file in the tree, display its contents in terminal

pending.title: Split panels.css into per-panel files
pending.description: src/renderer/styles/components/panels.css has grown to 4339 lines and now contains styles for every panel (history, tasks, plugins, github, overview, structure map, prompts, spotlight). Split into per-panel files (e.g. tasks-panel.css, github-panel.css, plugins-panel.css, history-panel.css, overview-panel.css, structure-map.css, prompts-panel.css, spotlight.css) and import them from main.css. Shared panel chrome (headers, collapse buttons, common modal patterns) stays in a smaller panels-shared.css.
pending.userRequest: User asked for total LOC of the project on 2026-04-26; in the report panels.css stood out as a 4339-line monolith and the user said: tasklara ekleyebilir misin? — referring to the suggestion of splitting it.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: panels.css is replaced by ~7 panel-scoped CSS files plus a panels-shared.css; main.css imports all of them in a sensible order; no visual regressions in any panel; total CSS line count is roughly the same (no dead code introduced).
pending.notes: Pure structural refactor, zero behavior change. Good candidate to do alongside the microcopy/string-extraction task since both are sweeps across the renderer surface. Should NOT be bundled with feature work.
pending.context: Session 2026-04-26 - LOC review surfaced panels.css as a monolith

pending.title: Custom keybinding remap UI
pending.description: Allow users to remap any registered shortcut from a Settings > Keybindings screen. Persist to a keybindings.json. Conflict detection. Builds on the central action registry from Command Palette.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Keybindings lists every action; user can record a new keystroke; conflicts are flagged; reset-to-default works; persists across restarts.
pending.notes: Hard requirement for power users (Vim/Emacs-style preferences). Depends on action registry.

pending.title: Empty-state design pass
pending.description: Design and implement consistent empty states across the app: no projects, no tasks, no PRs, no plugins, no commits, no terminals. Each empty state should have an illustration/icon, one-line context, and a primary call-to-action.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Every list/panel in the app has a designed empty state with CTA; visual style is consistent; copy is reviewed.
pending.notes: Empty states are where the product's character shows the most.

pending.title: Window & layout state persistence
pending.description: Restore on launch: window size & position, last opened project, panel visibility (sidebar, github, plugins, history, tasks), terminal grid layout, active terminal, theme. Centralize layout state into a single workspace-state.json managed in main.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Closing and reopening the app brings back the exact prior state; per-project layout memory; works across multi-monitor setups.
pending.notes: Sidebar-hidden state already persists in localStorage but it's fragmented — unify everything.

pending.title: Crash reporting (Sentry / Bugsnag)
pending.description: Integrate a crash reporter in both main and renderer. Capture stack traces, app version, OS, anonymized session id. Opt-in toggle in Settings, transparent privacy notice.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Renderer JS errors and main process uncaught exceptions reach the dashboard; PII scrubbed; user can disable in Settings; first-run prompt asks for consent.
pending.notes: Without this, production bugs are invisible. Sentry's Electron SDK covers both processes.

pending.title: Opt-in usage telemetry
pending.description: Anonymous, opt-in event tracking (PostHog or Plausible-style). Track: feature usage (which panels open, which AI tools chosen, terminal counts, grid layouts used), session duration, retention. No prompt content, no file paths, no user data.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Telemetry off by default; clear opt-in; events documented in PRIVACY.md; dashboard answers 'which features are used vs dead'.
pending.notes: Critical for data-driven roadmap decisions. Combined with crash reporting it forms the operational visibility layer.

pending.title: Auto-update UX polish
pending.description: On top of existing electron-updater (or equivalent) flow: in-app 'update available' banner, release notes shown before install, restart prompt, version badge in UI tied to changelog. Currently version is shown but the update flow is invisible.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: User sees update banner with release notes; can defer; restart-to-install works; failed updates surface clearly; no silent failures.
pending.notes: Pairs with in-app changelog / What's New.

pending.title: Diagnostic / log bundle export
pending.description: Help > Export Diagnostics generates a zip with main+renderer logs, app version, OS, settings (sans secrets), recent error events. Pre-attached when the user files a bug.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Single click produces a zip; secrets/tokens scrubbed; bundled file is opened in Finder/Explorer; documented in CONTRIBUTING.
pending.notes: Multiplies bug-report quality from users — fewer 'doesn't work for me' tickets.

pending.title: Brand consistency audit
pending.description: Audit and unify visual identity across: app icon, splash screen, dock badge, tray icon, notification icon, favicon, README hero, frame.cool landing, OG images, social preview. Single brand kit (logo, type, color tokens) consumed by both app and landing.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: All surfaces use the same logo/colors/typography; brand kit lives in one place (e.g. /brand or /assets/brand); landing and app feel like the same product.
pending.notes: Cheap but high-impact; first impression in App Store-style listings depends on it.

pending.title: Microcopy & tone-of-voice pass
pending.description: Sweep every user-facing string: error messages, empty states, tooltips, button labels, dialog titles. Establish a tone-of-voice guide and rewrite inconsistencies. Centralize strings to ease future i18n.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: All strings reviewed; tone guide documented; obvious inconsistencies fixed; strings extracted to a single module.
pending.notes: String extraction here is the natural prerequisite for i18n.

pending.title: i18n infrastructure (TR / EN)
pending.description: Set up i18n framework (i18next or similar). Extract all strings to locale files. Ship TR + EN initially. Language selector in Settings; default from OS locale.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Switching language updates all UI without restart; missing keys fall back to EN; new strings added in code automatically appear in default locale file.

pending.title: Accessibility pass (a11y)
pending.description: Keyboard-only navigation through every panel; visible focus rings; ARIA roles/labels; color contrast audit; screen-reader smoke test (VoiceOver). Particularly important since the user base is dev-tool focused — many use keyboard exclusively.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Every interactive element reachable via Tab; visible focus state; AXE audit passes with no critical issues; basic VoiceOver test completes happy path.
pending.notes: Quick wins early (focus rings, ARIA on buttons); deeper screen-reader work later.

pending.title: Detach panel / multi-monitor support
pending.description: Allow popping a terminal or panel out into a separate window. Especially valuable for multi-monitor power users running long agents on one screen and editing on another.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Right-click a tab/panel > Detach; new BrowserWindow opens with shared IPC state; closing detaches re-attaches or disposes cleanly; window position remembered per project.
pending.notes: Larger architectural change — needs IPC state sharing across windows. Park for later but useful to have on the list.

pending.title: Account & settings sync layer
pending.description: Identity layer (email + magic link or OAuth). Sync settings, project list, keybindings across devices. Foundation for paid tier even if launch is free.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Sign-in flow works; settings sync verified across two devices; signed-out users keep current local-only experience.
pending.notes: Adding identity later costs ~10x more than designing for it now. Even if not exposed in MVP, the abstractions matter.

pending.title: License / entitlement layer
pending.description: Foundation for free vs pro tiers: feature flags gated by entitlement, license validation hook. No actual paid features yet — just the plumbing.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: isFeatureEnabled(flag) API exists; default 'all free'; entitlement source configurable (local file / remote); covered by tests.
pending.notes: Depends on account/sync. Premature optimization risk — only build when monetization is on the roadmap.

pending.title: Privacy policy, EULA & terms
pending.description: Author Privacy Policy (covers telemetry, crash reports, account data), EULA, Terms of Service. Link from About dialog and frame.cool footer.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Three docs exist in repo and on landing; About dialog links to them; first-run telemetry consent references the privacy doc.
pending.notes: Legal review recommended before paid tier or App Store distribution.

pending.title: E2E test infrastructure (Playwright)
pending.description: Set up Playwright for Electron with a smoke suite: launch app, open project, start AI session, open each panel, create/close terminal, verify no console errors. Run on PRs.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Smoke suite runs locally and in CI; failure blocks PR merge; <2 min runtime; covers happy path of core flows.
pending.notes: Manual QA matrix is unsustainable as panels and AI tools multiply. Even a thin smoke suite catches most regressions.

pending.title: In-app changelog / What's New
pending.description: On version bump, show a 'What's New' modal with the latest release notes. Reachable any time from Help menu. Source from a structured CHANGELOG.md.
pending.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim — 2026-04-26 product audit.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Updating to a new version surfaces release notes once; user can dismiss; reopen via Help; CHANGELOG.md is the single source.

inProgress.title: Search in files
inProgress.description: Basic file search functionality within project

inProgress.title: Resizable sidebar
inProgress.description: Allow users to drag and resize the sidebar width

inProgress.title: System prompt standardization
inProgress.description: Standardize all system-generated prompts (task delegation, automated commands) to English. Create a centralized prompt template system. frame projesinde oluşturulan içerideki dökümanlarımız, claude md, readme, projectnotes md dosyaları ingilizce olmalı. şu an bu projede bulunan içerik türkçeyse içerik anlamını kaybetmeden ingilizceye çevrilmeli. bundan sonra initialize edilen projelerde bu dosyalarda hazırladığımız direktifler ise ingilizce olarak gelmeli.

inProgress.title: Add reverse dependency analysis to intentIndex
inProgress.description: Extend update-structure.js to track which modules import each file (reverse dependencies). Currently the 'depends' field only captures outgoing imports. Adding reverse lookups would let the intentIndex surface implicit coupling between differently-named modules — e.g. a React component and the API route it calls. Implementation: after parsing all modules, build a reverse map (file → who imports it) and store it as 'usedBy' per module in STRUCTURE.json.
inProgress.userRequest: User said: bunu düşünebiliriz evet, istersen tasklara ekleyelim — following a HN comment about implicit coupling not being captured by the current naming-based intentIndex.
inProgress.acceptanceCriteria: Each module in STRUCTURE.json has a 'usedBy' array listing modules that import it. find-module.js deep search (tier 3) also searches usedBy relationships. Tested on at least one cross-feature dependency case.
inProgress.notes: Dynamic code and reflection are out of scope — those can't be resolved statically. This only covers explicit import/require coupling. Embeddings were discussed but ruled out due to cost and complexity.
inProgress.context: Session 2026-03-19 - HN post discussion on intentIndex edge cases

completed.title: Git status integration (file tree decoration)
completed.description: v1: decorate the sidebar file tree with git status colors. New gitStatusManager (main) runs `git status --porcelain=v1 -z` for the active project on a 5s poll, parses output, pushes to renderer via GIT_STATUS_DATA. Renderer maps each file's relative path to a status classification (modified / added / untracked / deleted / renamed / conflict / ignored) and applies a CSS class to the .file-item. Folders containing any changed descendant get a 'git-has-changes' rollup class. Diff viewer, staged-files panel, and stage/discard actions are explicitly out of scope for v1 — they are v2.
completed.userRequest: User said: task m2 çok eklenmesi gerekli bir özellik ya, istersen onları da ekleyelim — 2026-04-28.
completed.acceptanceCriteria: File tree shows colored decorations matching git status; folder rollup works; status refreshes every ~5s; non-git projects render unchanged; switching projects updates the watch target.
completed.notes: Polling chosen over fs.watch for v1 simplicity. Diff viewer + Changes panel deferred to v2. Visual style is colored text only (VSCode pattern), no badges, to keep tree clean. Identical pushes are de-duplicated to avoid renderer churn.

completed.title: Filter input in file tree (substring match)
completed.description: Search box above the file tree that filters visible items as the user types. Substring (case-insensitive) match against file/folder names. Folders containing matches auto-expand to surface results. Clearing the input restores full visibility. Keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/Down) automatically skips hidden items via offsetParent check. Filter is re-applied automatically when the tree reloads (project switch / refresh).
completed.userRequest: User said: bir de search box koyalım. dosyalarda arama yapılabilsin — 2026-04-28 file tree polish.
completed.acceptanceCriteria: Input above tree filters in real-time; substring match (case-insensitive); folders with matching descendants auto-expand; clearing input restores tree; keyboard nav skips hidden items.
completed.notes: Substring chosen over fuzzy because fuzzy match in a file tree filter occasionally surprises users. A fuzzy 'Quick Open' (Cmd+P style file finder across all files) is a separate future feature.

completed.title: Right-click → Copy Filepath in file tree
completed.description: Custom HTML context menu that appears on right-click of file or folder items. Single menu item: 'Copy Filepath' which writes the absolute path to the clipboard via Electron's clipboard module. Menu dismisses on outside click, Esc, scroll, window blur, or after action. Auto-clamps to viewport on right/bottom edges.
completed.userRequest: User said: filetree de bir dosyaya sağ tıkladığımda copy filepath olsun mesela, bu güzel bir özellik — 2026-04-28 file tree polish.
completed.acceptanceCriteria: Right-click on any file or folder shows context menu at cursor; 'Copy Filepath' writes absolute path to clipboard; menu closes on outside-click / Esc / after action.
completed.notes: Custom HTML menu (not Electron native Menu.popup) so future items can match Frame's design tokens. Works for both files and folders for consistency.

completed.title: First-run welcome flow with persistent dismiss
completed.description: Single-screen welcome modal shown on every launch unless user opts out via 'Don't show this again' checkbox. Pitches Frame's value prop (terminal-first, AI-native), offers three primary actions (Open Folder / Create New / Clone GitHub), embeds AI tool radio picker synced with sidebar via SET_AI_TOOL IPC, has Close primary button + Skip secondary. Reachable later via Command Palette (help.welcome). Persistence moved from localStorage to userData JSON via new userSettings module + IPC channels because Electron renderer localStorage didn't survive launches in dev. Checkbox state persists immediately on change so Cmd+Q while modal open is still captured.
completed.userRequest: User said: first-run welcome flow u entegre etmeliyiz bence, önemli bir ux iyileştirmesi olur — followed by: ben modali çok beğendim. ilk açılışta her zaman açılsın. dont show this again seçeneği koyalım — followed by: belki bir de close diye bi buton da koyalım modalin alt orta kısmına.
completed.acceptanceCriteria: Modal opens on every launch by default; AI tool radio cards in sync with sidebar selector; checkbox state persisted instantly to userData JSON; Close / Skip / Esc / outside-click all dismiss; Cmd+Q while open still saves; reachable via Command Palette > Show Welcome Screen with flag reset.
completed.notes: Discovered and fixed an Electron localStorage persistence quirk during testing — switched to a generic userSettings IPC pattern that mirrors aiToolManager's userData JSON approach. New IPC channels GET_USER_SETTING / SET_USER_SETTING are reusable for future renderer-side preferences (telemetry consent, theme, etc.).

completed.title: App boot loader splash
completed.description: Full-screen centered loader shown on app boot until the first WORKSPACE_DATA IPC arrives (or a 10s failsafe). Pulsing brand mark (sparkle ✦) + 'Loading workspace…' caption, fades out with CSS transition then removes itself from DOM. Avoids the brief flash of empty sidebar / unmounted terminal users with many projects experience during initial workspace load. Listener registered before welcomeOverlay's so the loader fades out before the welcome modal can appear behind it.
completed.userRequest: User said: bir ilk açılışta mesela benim artık bir sürü projem olduğu için bir kaç saniye projelerin dolmasını bekliyorum. acaba bu bekleme zamanı için bir loading animasyonu gibi bişey ekleyebilir miyiz? — followed by: sidebarda değil aslında, tam ortada görmek istiyorum.
completed.acceptanceCriteria: Loader visible on boot covering the whole window; fades out within ~280ms once workspace data arrives; never traps user (10s failsafe); welcome modal opens cleanly behind it.
completed.notes: Initially tried a sidebar skeleton placeholder, but user preferred a centered full-screen approach. Z-index 100000 layers above all panels; failsafe handles the unlikely case where WORKSPACE_DATA never arrives.

completed.title: In-app keyboard shortcut cheat sheet
completed.description: Cmd+Shift+K overlay listing every registered shortcut grouped by category (AI, Focus, Help, Palette, Panel, Project, Terminal). Reads from commandRegistry so it stays in sync with the Command Palette automatically. Filter input matches title, category, and rendered shortcut. Layout-independent binding (avoids the TR-keyboard issue with Cmd+/).
completed.userRequest: User said: tamam devam edelim — after recommending the cheat sheet as the highest-leverage follow-up to the command palette work.
completed.acceptanceCriteria: Cmd+Shift+K opens overlay; categories visible; searchable; OS-correct symbols (⌘ vs Ctrl); reachable via Command Palette (help.shortcuts entry).
completed.notes: Originally proposed Cmd+/ but on TR-Q keyboards / requires Shift+7, making the combo awkward and our matcher refuses Shift modifier — so binding moved to Cmd+Shift+K (mnemonic: Keyboard, layout-independent on TR/US/UK/DE).

completed.title: Command Palette (Cmd+P / Cmd+Shift+P)
completed.description: Universal fuzzy-search palette to trigger every app action: open project, switch terminal, toggle panels, run shortcuts, open settings, switch AI tool, etc. Solves discoverability and the 'I closed a panel and can't reopen it' problem at its root. Implementation: central commandRegistry as single source of truth (id, title, shortcut, run, when), commandPalette modal UI, platform helper for cross-platform shortcut formatting (CmdOrCtrl accelerator -> ⌘⇧P on macOS, Ctrl+Shift+P on Windows/Linux). All existing keyboard shortcuts migrated through the registry.
completed.userRequest: User said: çok güzel öneriler, bence hepsini tasks.json a ekleyelim ve bir yerden bunları eklemeye başlayalım — captured during 2026-04-26 product readiness audit.
completed.acceptanceCriteria: Cmd+P opens palette; all toggleable panels, terminal actions, AI tool switches, project switches are reachable; recent actions surface first; Esc closes; full keyboard navigation; new modules can register commands via a single API.
completed.notes: Plugins panel shortcut moved from Cmd+Shift+P to Cmd+Shift+X (VSCode 'Extensions' convention) to free up Cmd+Shift+P for the palette. Cmd+Shift+G previously double-fired (terminal grid view + GitHub panel) — resolved by binding Cmd+Shift+G only to GitHub panel; grid view is reachable via tab bar and palette. Focus commands (Cmd+E / Cmd+Shift+E) now reveal the corresponding sidebar tab and open the sidebar if hidden.
